title: Evangelion Super Robo or Real Robo
display_title: Evangelion Super Robo or Real Robo
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion: Super Robo or Real Robo?             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Mon Jul 18, 2005 7:16 am Before you say anything, YES i know they're technically not robo at all. More like cyborgs, actually. Would you consider Eva a Super Robo anime (Mazingers, Combattler & Voltez V, Getta Robos, etc.) or a "Real" Robo anime (Gundam, Macross, Patlabor, etc.)? I, personally, am undecided. It seems to encorporate elements of both. Perhaps it doesn't deserve to be shoehorned into one category or another, but let's try just for the sake of argument, eh? One the one hand we have... SUPAA ROBO: -Teenaged pilot's father is the creator of the mecha (Gendo). -Giant monster enemies, each posessing its own special "gimmick" (Ramiel's cannon, Israfel's "Geminiman" move, etc.). -Giant mecha have flashy, primary coloured paintjobs. -Godlike fictional energy sources (S2 organ). -Underground cities (Tokyo 3). -Teenaged pilot's female love interest is a fellow pilot (2 of them are, actually. More on that later). -Teenaged pilot still tries to go to skool & lead a semi-normal life in between attempts to save the world. -Team up attacks (Both of you dance like you want to win). -Creepy, inhuman looking evil overlord guy antagonist (Kiel). -Goofy looking, inferior "Joke Robo" (Jet Alone). -Only certain people can pilot the mecha (this is true of a lot of "Real Robo series, too, though). -Custom pilot suits that match the mecha's paintjob (although Rei & Shinji lack this). -Giant, mystical ancient weapon (Lance o' Longinus). -The mecha have souls. -Religeous references (Brave Raideen did Egyptian stuff, Steel Jeeg did ancient Japanese Shinto stuff, various others have took some inspiration from eastern mysticism or mythical stuff, now Eva's doing Judeo-Christian stuff). There's probably more, but that's all I can think of for now. And of course there's the more realistic stuff... REAL ROBO: -Political intrigue, conspiracy & espionage. -Moral ambiguity (you could even say SEELE are admirable for trying to end human suffering...albeit in a very twisted way). -Semi-realistic weapons & gadgets (Most of the guns are based on real one, only bigger & even the mighty positron guns are based on scientific principles that have been tested & proven in the lab). -Use of industrial terms to describe the mecha (Prototype, Test type, Production type & Massproduction type). -Army of mass produced grunt mecha based on the title robo (The 05s, although, unlike most masspro mecha which are usually cheaper watered down versions of the prototype, these are probably more fearsome than the beasts they were cloned from). -Teenaged pilot is angsty & reluctant (Let's face it, Shinji's emo-ness puts even Amuro Ray in his early days to shame. He's about as far from the typical gung-ho Super Robo pilot as you can get). -Teenaged pilot has multiple potential female love interests (Super Robo pilots in the old days were fairly monogamous. It was always Koji & Sayaka, Tetsuya & June, Hyouma & Chizuru. Godannar's pilots are even married! It wasn't until Gundam came along that we started to see the hero forming bonds with many different girls. Amuro had Fraw, Matilda, Sayla & Lalah Sune, & that's just in the first series. Also interesting to note is that while the real robo pilot has more choices for a prospective girlfriend, he also has a harder time getting close to any of them). -Character designs (The way the characters are portrayed is very different in super & real robo shows. This is often most visible in the female characters. Super robo heroines, especially from older shows, are often more of the "girl next door" type than the flashy "Bishoujo" characters you see in real robo anime. Ironic really, considering the real robo heroines are generally less realistic looking than their super robot counterparts). -Realistic energy sources & limitations for the mecha (how many other mecha shows require that the starring robos be plugged into a wall socket with an extension cord to work?). -Psychologically/emotionally damaged heros (I think having some sort of inner darkness is one of the requirements to get your Real Robo pilot's license). Wow...that was long. Anyway, hope that was entertaining. Tell me what you think. Anything to add? Did I completely miss the point of anything?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Bud » Mon Jul 18, 2005 10:05 am ........... Super what?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Mon Jul 18, 2005 10:40 am You'd be better off raising this topic on a site like GameFAQs. The philistines around here don't play SRW, so they can't help.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Jul 18, 2005 12:30 pm SRW? philistines?

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Mon Jul 18, 2005 4:20 pm the show is not about robots though, they just happend to be in there. its a more of a character relationship show, with all sorts of stuff mixed into it... like rainbow chip cookies.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Mon Jul 18, 2005 4:39 pm sadsadshinji wrote: SRW?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Tue Jul 19, 2005 3:05 am Ryo wrote: sadsadshinji wrote: SRW?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Jul 19, 2005 9:58 am is this an RPG or a computer game type of thing? i have never heard of it

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Tue Jul 19, 2005 12:33 pm sadsadshinji wrote: is this an RPG or a computer game type of thing? i have never heard of it

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Tue Jul 19, 2005 5:52 pm damn the Japanese for not thinking about us selfish english-hardcore fans!!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Jul 19, 2005 6:46 pm if i will eventually learn any Japanese, it will be kanji (that is the set of characters that is virtually identical to chinese, correct?) sounds like those are fun, but i suspect that eBay would be the sole source of them?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Tue Jul 19, 2005 6:58 pm sadsadshinji wrote: if i will eventually learn any Japanese, it will be kanji (that is the set of characters that is virtually identical to chinese, correct?) sounds like those are fun, but i suspect that eBay would be the sole source of them?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 19, 2005 10:13 pm Do they really have the rights over every and all merchandise pertaining to the mecha in what they liscenced? They got the rights to make and distribute their crappy domestic DVDs. That doesn't mean that every item with "Evangelion" on it goes through them.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Tue Jul 19, 2005 10:17 pm Reichu wrote: Do they really have the rights over every and all merchandise pertaining to the mecha in what they liscenced? They got the rights to make and distribute their crappy domestic DVDs. That doesn't mean that every item with "Evangelion" on it goes through them.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 19, 2005 10:42 pm Sounds like bullshit to me... If that were true, this Mangle thing couldn't have happened, and local companies wouldn't be localizing the Kaiyodo action figs without slapping some "ADV" sticker onto them... Heck, Kaiyodo has even made two (rather ugly) NGE 'action figures' specifically with the Amerikajin in mind (IIRC). Nope, just a "GAINAX" sticker on that. They just have the rights to publish and distribute episodes #01 thru #26. Mangle has the rights for the two movies. I don't see how either of them could be entitled to dough pertaining to anything BUT what they specifically own. That money's for Gainax, and Gainax alone.

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Tue Jul 19, 2005 11:01 pm Reichu wrote: Sounds like bullshit to me... If that were true, this Mangle thing couldn't have happened, and local companies wouldn't be localizing the Kaiyodo action figs without slapping some "ADV" sticker onto them... Heck, Kaiyodo has even made two (rather ugly) NGE 'action figures' specifically with the Amerikajin in mind (IIRC). Nope, just a "GAINAX" sticker on that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jul 19, 2005 11:26 pm Or maybe I just don't know what I'm taking about. Something doesn't seem right, either way.

[]:             Quote Post by Iblis 19th Angel » Wed Jul 20, 2005 1:13 am Actually, you can get SRW3 in english. There's a ROM hack that translates it. They're also working on the rest. I think the hack for SRW4 is coming out soon. I wish they'd do 1 & 2 first...

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Wed Jul 20, 2005 2:28 am Iblis 19th Angel wrote: Actually, you can get SRW3 in english. There's a ROM hack that translates it. They're also working on the rest. I think the hack for SRW4 is coming out soon. I wish they'd do 1 & 2 first...
